ib.Average All Implemented Interfaces: Actor , java.lang.Cloneable, Debuggable , DebugListener , Executable , ModelErrorHandler , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor public class Average extends Transformer Output the average of the inputs af

ds Transformer Output the average of the inputs after the last time a true token is received at the reset port. One output is produced each time the actor is fired. The inputs and outputs can be any token type that supports addition and division by a

oken type that supports addition and division by an integer. The output type is constrained to be the same as the input type. Note that the type system will fail to catch some errors. Static type checking may result in a resolved type that does not

cking may result in a resolved type that does not support addition and division. In this case, a run-time error will occur. Since: Ptolemy II 0.3 Version: $Id: Average.java,v 1.31 2003/01/08 01:20:16 ptII Exp $ Author: Edward A. Lee, Jie Liu See Als

the averaging process will be reset. Constructor Detail Average public Average ( CompositeEntity container, java.lang.String name) throws NameDuplicationException , IllegalActionException Construct an actor with the given co

tionException Construct an actor with the given container and name. Parameters: container - The container. name - The name of this actor. Throws: IllegalActionException - If the actor cannot be contained by the proposed container. NameDuplicationE

ned by the proposed container. NameDuplicationException - If the container already has an actor with this name. Method Detail fire public void fire () throws IllegalActionException Consume at most one token from the input and compute

sume at most one token from the input and compute the average of the input tokens so far. Send the result to the output. If there is no input token available, no output will be produced. If there is a true-valued token on the reset input, the

e is a true-valued token on the reset input, then the average is reset, and the output will be equal to the input token (if there is one). If the fire method is invoked multiple times in one iteration, then only the input read on the last i

teration, then only the input read on the last invocation in the iteration will affect future averages. Inputs that are read earlier in the iteration are forgotten. Specified by: fire in interface Executable Overrides: fire in class AtomicActo

ace Executable Overrides: fire in class AtomicActor Throws: IllegalActionException - If addition or division by an integer are not supported by the supplied tokens. initialize public void initialize () throws IllegalActionException
